item.title: JWST Cycle 6 Call for Proposals - NASA Science
item.description: We are pleased to announce the Cycle 6 Call for Proposals for JWST General Observer (GO), Survey, and Archival Research (AR) programs. Proposals are due on 30 September 2026.

item.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support - NASA
item.description: Following its liftoff from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) hosting the NASA-supported Robotic

item.title: Join the Jam on Proxima b - NASA Science
item.description: Tidally Locked and Ready to Rock! Only 4.2 Light-Years From Earth! It's always golden hour at the Perpetual Sunset Stadium on Proxima Centauri b. This planet

item.title: Mapping Io’s Hidden Heat With NASA’s Juno - NASA Science
item.description: This graphic illustrates the areas of Jupiter’s moon Io sampled by the Microwave Radiometer (MWR) instrument aboard NASA’s Juno spacecraft during two close

item.title: NASA’s Juno Peers Beneath Io’s Surface - NASA Science
item.description: This map represents data captured by the Microwave Radiometer (MWR) aboard NASA’s Juno spacecraft, indicating heat rising from just beneath the surface of

item.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia - NASA
item.description: Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the

item.title: Weekend Departure Preps Continue as Crew Works Science, Spacesuits - NASA
item.description: Three Expedition 74 crew members are nearing the end of their stay aboard the International Space Station handing over mission responsibilities and packing up cargo for return to Earth. Science hardware, lab maintenance, and spacesuits kept the rest of the crew busy aboard the orbital outpost.

item.title: Darryl Gaines - NASA
item.description: Darryl Gaines currently serves as acting deputy director of the Exploration Architecture Integration and Science Directorate

item.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return - NASA
item.description: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and Roscosmos cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev are wrapping up their 241‑day mission aboard the International

item.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94 - NASA
item.description: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory unveiled this new look at the galaxy NGC 4736, also known as Messier 94, on June 30, 2026. Messier 94 is a spiral galaxy with
